info.title: SEL Platform Backend - Agent Discovery & Workflow Orchestration
info.description: Backend API for SEL agent discovery, orchestration, and chat interactions. 🤖✨ Features include: 🔍 Agent discovery & management, 💬 Real-time chat with AI agents, 🔄 Workflow orchestration, 🛠️ Tool integration via MCP servers, and 🔐 User authentication & session management.

get.description: API root with service information.
Health check endpoint.

Get all available LLM model configurations.

Returns model specifications including token limits and pricing.

post.description:
Upload a file to the session workspace.

Requires a valid session_id to organize uploaded files.

Returns the absolute path to the saved file.

Refresh AWS Bedrock client credentials.

Call this endpoint after updating AWS credentials externally (e.g., via MFA assume-role)
to make the service use the new credentials without requiring a service restart.

⚠️ **Note**: This requires AWS Bedrock to be configured as the LLM provider.

Returns:
    dict: Status of the credential refresh operation
Authenticate with user_id + password and receive tokens.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token.
Create the first admin user. Only works when no admin exists yet.
Change own password. Requires current password verification.
Logout by revoking a specific refresh token.

get.description: Return the currently authenticated user's profile.

post.description: Reset a user's password (admin only). Forces must_change on next login.

delete.description: Delete a user (admin only). Sessions/workflow data is preserved.

get.description: List all registered users (admin only).

Get list of all available SEL agents (minimal details).

Returns only the keys defined in minimal-agent-listing-keys.

Get the JSON Schema for agent-card format.

Returns the agent-card-schema.json which defines the structure
and validation rules for agent card metadata.

Get full details for a specific agent.

Includes:
- Full agent-card.json
- details.md content
- mcp.json configuration
- openapi.json specification

post.description:
a SEL Agent.

Supports multiple agent types:
- 'discovery': Agent discovery and recommendation
- 'orchestrator': Multi-agent workflow orchestration

get.description:
Get LLM token usage quota for all models.

Returns usage statistics for all models in the system.

delete.description:
Delete a session and cleanup resources.

Deletes session from both in-memory cache (if loaded) and Redis persistence.

get.description:
List all sessions for the authenticated user (from Redis).

Returns both active (in-memory) and persisted (Redis) sessions.

post.description:
Cancel a running agent query for a session.

This will gracefully stop the agent's current operation.

get.description: Get the current task status for a session.
Get all messages from a specific session in chronological order.

Get list of tools with optional filtering.

Requires authentication via Bearer token.

Query Parameters:
- agent: Filter by agent name (e.g., 'discovery', 'orchestrator', 'common')
- category: Filter by category (e.g., 'agent_discovery', 'code_execution')
- enabled_only: Only return enabled tools (default: True)

post.description:
Create and start a new orchestrated workflow.

This endpoint:
1. Creates a new workflow
2. Generates workflow plan using LLM
3. Starts workflow execution (async)
4. Returns workflow ID and initial status

The workflow runs asynchronously and sends progress updates via WebSocket.

get.description: Get workflow status and details.

delete.description:
Delete a workflow and all its associated data from Redis.

This endpoint permanently removes a workflow including:
- Workflow state
- Approval data
- Evolution events
- Phase reviews
- Turn reviews

**Restrictions:**
- Cannot delete workflows that are currently running
- Must be the workflow owner

**Use Cases:**
- Clean up completed workflows
- Remove cancelled workflows
- Delete failed workflows after investigation

get.description:
Get workflow evolution history and audit trail.

This endpoint returns a detailed audit trail of how the workflow
evolved during execution, including:
- Phases that were skipped and why
- Phases that were modified and how
- Phases that were added dynamically
- Phase reviews with completion and quality scores
- Timeline of all evolution events

Use this to understand:
- Why certain phases were skipped (redundancy detected)
- How the workflow adapted based on phase results
- Quality metrics for each completed phase

List workflows for the current user.

**Legacy mode** (no `page` param): Returns List[WorkflowState] — full objects, same as before.
**Paginated mode** (with `page` param): Returns lightweight summaries with pagination, stats, and optional search/filter.

parameters.name: page

schema.description: Page number (1-based). Omit for legacy full-list response.
schema.title: Page

parameters.description: Page number (1-based). Omit for legacy full-list response.

schema.title: Response List Workflows Api Orchestrator Workflows Get

post.description:
Resume a failed or cancelled workflow from its last checkpoint.

The workflow will:
1. Load state from the file-based checkpoint system
2. Reset any failed phases to pending status
3. Continue execution from where it left off

Note: Only FAILED or CANCELLED workflows can be resumed.

get.description: List all available workflow checkpoints for the current user.

schema.title: Response List Checkpoints Api Orchestrator Checkpoints Get

post.description:
Cancel a running workflow.

Sets the workflow status to CANCELLED which will be detected by the
execution loop and stop further phase execution. Already-running phases
may complete before the cancellation takes effect.

get.description:
Get all phases with detailed status for a workflow.

Returns enriched phase data including:
- Phase execution status
- Turn details for multi-turn phases
- Duration and token usage
- Results and artifacts
- **Phase reviews** with quality scores, accomplishments, gaps
- **Turn reviews** with detailed analysis per turn

Get all artifacts generated during workflow execution.

Scans the session_workdir for markdown files and returns their contents.
Also includes any artifacts stored in workflow state.

Artifacts include:
- Generated markdown documentation
- Analysis results
- Plans and proposals
- Any other .md files created by coding-agent

Note: Excludes library/framework directories like node_modules, __pycache__, venv, etc.

Get agent invocation history for a workflow.

Returns detailed log of all agent calls including:
- coding-agent invocations with personas
- Duration and token usage per invocation
- Success/failure status
- Phase and turn context

Get comprehensive performance metrics for a workflow.

Returns:
- Total execution time
- Token usage breakdown
- Cost estimation
- Phase-level metrics with quality scores
- Turn-level metrics
- Adaptation efficiency metrics
- Quality summary from phase reviews

post.description:
Approve a workflow that is pending approval.

This endpoint:
1. Validates workflow exists and is in PENDING_APPROVAL state
2. Updates the approval status in Redis
3. Resumes workflow execution

Reject a workflow that is pending approval.

This will:
1. Update the approval status to rejected
2. Set the workflow status to CANCELLED

get.description: Get approval status for a workflow.

Get LLM-based scoring feedback for a completed workflow.

Returns 200 with scoring data on cache hit, or 202 if scoring is being
generated in the background (frontend should poll until ready).

Parameters:
- refresh: If True, forces re-calculation of scoring (clears cache first)

List all pending approvals for the current user.

Queries workflows in PENDING_APPROVAL status from Redis.
Use /workflow/{workflow_id}/approve or /workflow/{workflow_id}/reject to respond.

Get aggregated leaderboard scores from all valid workflow executions.

This endpoint provides comprehensive performance analytics across all workflows:

**Sections Included:**

1. **Agent Performance**: Per-agent scores with min, max, average, median, std deviation
   - Groups by agent type (coding-agent, code-miner, knowledge-mining-agent)
   - Includes invocation counts, success rates, and duration metrics

2. **Persona Performance**: Per-persona scores with min, max, average
   - Normalizes mcp_tool -> architect for consistency
   - Tracks persona effectiveness across workflows

3. **Phase Performance**: Grouped by phase_type (context_bootstrap, execute, etc.)
   - Aggregates similar phases across all workflows
   - Includes breakdown scores (completeness, correctness, quality, efficiency)

4. **Workflow Performance**: Overall workflow score statistics
   - Min, max, average of workflow overall_scores
   - Phase and turn average statistics

5. **Turn Performance**: Turn-level scoring analytics
   - Overall and by-persona breakdowns

6. **System Health**: Composite health score and status

7. **Quality Distribution**: Score distribution across quality tiers

8. **Insights**: AI-generated actionable insights and recommendations

**Parameters:**
- `workflow_ids`: Comma-separated list of specific workflow IDs to include
- `human_verified_ids`: Comma-separated workflow IDs that bypass status checks
- `include_incomplete`: Include workflows not in completed status

**Note:** Only completed workflows are considered by default, unless included
in human_verified_ids or include_incomplete is set to true.
